claim_boundary: This casefile assembles evidence and authority state. It does not turn model output into repair authority; production release requires measured evidence, terminal outcome, and a passing authority ledger.

design_test_kit.claim_boundary: This test kit can validate requirements, module contracts, evidence gates, and supplied DC power-tree netlists. It does not authorize arbitrary unknown boards, mains work, battery packs, or production release without measured topology, bench evidence, and specialist review where hazards apply.

field_model_advisory.prompt_preview:
You are Circuit-AI's live field advisory model.
Return ONLY one compact JSON object.
Top-level keys: schema_version, field_call_alignment, measurement_priorities, qwen_evidence_uses, warnings, operator_prompt, structured_measurement_template, forbidden_claims.
schema_version must be field_model_advisory.v1.
Your job is to prioritize the next measurements for the selected field action.
Keep the answer compact: at most 6 measurement_priorities, 5 qwen_evidence_uses, 5 warnings, and 6 forbidden_claims.
Keep every string under 150 characters.
Use only labels, connectors, components, and tasks present in the context.
Do not invent pinouts, voltages, board revisions, exact part identities, safety clearance, or splice authority.
Do not say the board is safe, ready, powered, reusable, repair-cleared, or splice-ready.
If a connector/pin/rail is uncertain, label it candidate_only and require measurement.
measurement_priorities rows must include: priority, target, action, why, expected_evidence, stop_condition.
qwen_evidence_uses rows must include: evidence_label, use, limitation.
structured_measurement_template must be a JSON object the operator can fill in.
forbidden_claims must list claims the model refuses to make from current evidence.
Context:

actions: identify reusable block pins or wires from labels, continuity, and close-up photos
power the reused block from a current-limited supply at the lowest plausible rated voltage
verify the block output function before connecting it to any other module
mount the new build so wires have strain relief and cannot short
record a first-power test, thermal check, and final output function
keep strain relief on harvested wires and connectors
mount moving parts so wires cannot touch blades, gears, belts, rails, or hot surfaces
label every reused connector with voltage, polarity, and function after verification
exit_criteria: Wiring matches the selected-resource contract.
Strain relief, insulation, fusing/current limiting, and labels are present.
The build has passed visual inspection before power.
stages.objective: Assemble the selected resources according to the splice/build plan.

actions: Use current-limited supply at the lowest plausible safe voltage.
Record voltage, current, thermal behavior, output function, and stop condition response.
Disconnect immediately on unexpected current, heat, smell, smoke, or unstable behavior.
exit_criteria: Output function works under current limit.
No abnormal heat/current/smell/noise is observed.
Measured behavior matches the target build assumptions.
stages.objective: Apply first power or make an external splice only after authority and evidence gates allow it.

actions: Record decision: built, repaired, reused, sold, unsafe_hold, not_worth_it, or failed.

honesty: Catalog costs are rough planning estimates, not live quotes.
A selected salvaged or owned resource is not splice-ready until its evidence gates close.
Do not use blocked battery, mains, high-voltage, or failed-evidence resources as gap fillers.
next_actions: Pass selected resources into the splice/build planner for wiring, measurement, and first-power steps.

resource_interpretation.constrained: Prefer already-owned or salvaged resources and surface evidence gates before reuse.
resource_interpretation.core_rule: salvage, owned inventory, procurement, and designed blocks are scored as resources; the resource pool changes, not the engine.
resource_interpretation.hybrid: Reuse proven resources and buy only the gaps.
resource_interpretation.open_procurement: Prefer reliable procurable modules when they beat uncertain reuse.

do_not_connect_until: rail voltage is measured under current limit
battery or mains hazards are cleared
mechanical_steps: keep strain relief on harvested wires and connectors
mount moving parts so wires cannot touch blades, gears, belts, rails, or hot surfaces
label every reused connector with voltage, polarity, and function after verification
required_measurements: unpowered resistance between power and ground
continuity from connector ground to exposed ground
wiring_steps: identify reusable block pins or wires from labels, continuity, and close-up photos
power the reused block from a current-limited supply at the lowest plausible rated voltage
verify the block output function before connecting it to any other module
mount the new build so wires have strain relief and cannot short
record a first-power test, thermal check, and final output function

power-to-ground resistance suggests a short
load current exceeds the wire, connector, switch, driver, or supply rating
module overheats, smells, smokes, or pulls unexpected current
battery chemistry/protection is unknown or the pack is damaged

honesty: Catalog costs are rough planning estimates, not live quotes.
A selected salvaged or owned resource is not splice-ready until its evidence gates close.
Do not use blocked battery, mains, high-voltage, or failed-evidence resources as gap fillers.
next_actions: Pass selected resources into the splice/build planner for wiring, measurement, and first-power steps.

resource_interpretation.constrained: Prefer already-owned or salvaged resources and surface evidence gates before reuse.
resource_interpretation.core_rule: salvage, owned inventory, procurement, and designed blocks are scored as resources; the resource pool changes, not the engine.
resource_interpretation.hybrid: Reuse proven resources and buy only the gaps.
resource_interpretation.open_procurement: Prefer reliable procurable modules when they beat uncertain reuse.

wiring_steps: identify reusable block pins or wires from labels, continuity, and close-up photos
power the reused block from a current-limited supply at the lowest plausible rated voltage

mount the new build so wires have strain relief and cannot short
record a first-power test, thermal check, and final output function

honesty: Catalog costs are rough planning estimates, not live quotes.
A selected salvaged or owned resource is not splice-ready until its evidence gates close.
Do not use blocked battery, mains, high-voltage, or failed-evidence resources as gap fillers.
next_actions: Pass selected resources into the splice/build planner for wiring, measurement, and first-power steps.

resource_interpretation.constrained: Prefer already-owned or salvaged resources and surface evidence gates before reuse.
resource_interpretation.core_rule: salvage, owned inventory, procurement, and designed blocks are scored as resources; the resource pool changes, not the engine.
resource_interpretation.hybrid: Reuse proven resources and buy only the gaps.
resource_interpretation.open_procurement: Prefer reliable procurable modules when they beat uncertain reuse.

stop_conditions: input voltage or polarity cannot be identified
power-to-ground resistance suggests a short
load current exceeds the wire, connector, switch, driver, or supply rating
module overheats, smells, smokes, or pulls unexpected current
battery chemistry/protection is unknown or the pack is damaged
